codeReview.summary: The commit correctly updates README.md and .factory/library/frontend-handoff.md and the repo still exposes readiness on the /api/v1/catalog/items read surfaces, but the feature does not finish the hard-cut promised by its own description: multiple surviving setup-oriented docs still present /api/v1/catalog/products* and /api/v1/accounting/catalog/products* as canonical stock-bearing setup hosts.

issues.description: This packet still says stock-bearing product entry is canonical on `POST /api/v1/catalog/products` and later repeats `GET/POST /api/v1/catalog/products` as the only surviving operator-facing setup routes (lines 49-53). That directly contradicts the feature requirement and VAL-SETUP-001/002 to hard-cut setup guidance to `/api/v1/catalog/items`.

Shared library guidance still names `POST /api/v1/catalog/products` as the canonical product endpoint and line 42 still says newly created SKUs must be discoverable via the canonical `/api/v1/catalog/products` browse/search surface. Leaving this library state behind means future workers are still pointed at the retired setup host.

The manufacturing inventory flow doc still lists `GET/POST /api/v1/accounting/catalog/products` and `POST /api/v1/accounting/catalog/products/bulk-variants` as the canonical accounting-aware stock-bearing create/import path. This leaves exactly the stale `/api/v1/accounting/catalog/products*` setup guidance that the feature description said would be removed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared setup/canonicalization knowledge is still split across older packet docs and library notes that advertise `/api/v1/catalog/products*` or `/api/v1/accounting/catalog/products*` as current setup truth, so reviewers/workers can satisfy the narrow verification checklist while missing contradictory shared-state docs.

summary: Fail: the feature cleaned the README and frontend handoff, and the OpenAPI/readiness contract remains aligned to `/api/v1/catalog/items`, but it did not complete the promised setup-truth hard-cut because several surviving docs/shared-state files still direct setup users or future workers to `/api/v1/catalog/products*` and `/api/v1/accounting/catalog/products*`.
